faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Mineola home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in woolens, cashmere, or silk garments, webbing in closet corners, tiny beige or gold moths flying near wardrobes, and shed larval casings on shelves or carpets. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, area rugs, or stored blankets. Our Mineola technicians provide free inspections to confirm infestations and assess the extent of damage.

faqs.question: What's included in your clothes moth control service for Mineola properties?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection of all vulnerable areas, targeted treatment of infested closets and storage spaces, application of residual products to prevent re-infestation, steam treatment for delicate fabrics when needed, and detailed prevention recommendations. We also offer follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination and can treat both webbing and casemaking clothes moth species common in Long Island.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control cost between $550 and $5,000 in Mineola?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the severity of infestation, property size, number of affected rooms, and treatment methods required. A single-room treatment with minor infestation starts around $550, while extensive infestations affecting multiple closets, attics, or entire homes require more intensive treatment plans. Large estates or properties with valuable antique textiles may need specialized care that reaches the higher end of the range.
